sections.paragraph:
εὐγλωττία, ας, ἡ (s. γλῶσσα; Eur. et al; Maximus Tyr. 25, 2d) smoothness of tongue, with implication of ingratiating manner, glibness, fluency of speech Ro 16:18 v.l. (on this rdg. JNorth, NTS 42, ’96, 600f; cp. εὐλογία).—DELG s.v. γλῶχε.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 404
